title: schedulePressRelease flips the status to `scheduled` but nothing publishes it at that moment — the sweeper is a separate, unshipped slice
summary: The press schedule/unschedule endpoints are live and a release really does move to status='scheduled' with a future published_at. But the background sweeper that flips a scheduled release to published when its time arrives has NOT shipped — it belongs to a later slice, alongside embargo. So a client told 'your announcement will go out Tuesday at 9am' will find it still sitting in `scheduled` on Tuesday at 9:01. Until the sweeper lands, treat schedule as recording INTENT and call publishPressRelease when you actually want a release live. Related: there is no embargo method at all (no endpoint, nothing mints an embargo token), so a journalist-preview flow cannot be built on `embargo_until` today either.
references: app/api/v1/dashboard_press.py — POST /press/{id}/schedule + /unschedule exist and set status; no sweeper reads them
app/api/v1/dashboard_press.py:57 — 'nothing mints a token yet (slice 3.2 owns embargo)'
app/services/press_service.py:1151 — defers the embargo path to the same later slice
Press/Newsroom board eb7a03e5 — slice 3.2 'Embargo + scheduled publish' was still in the `plan` column when the agent surface (4.2) shipped
